module_title: ML for Engineers Module 0 - The Hook

questions.question_text: Which of the following is NOT one of the 5 ML demos shown in Module 0?
questions.explanation: The five demos in Module 0 are: stock predictor, cricket predictor, face detection, sentiment analyzer, and chatbot. Weather forecasting was not included.

questions.question_text: What is the primary advantage of using Google Colab for machine learning?
options: It has the best code editor available
Free GPU access in the cloud
It is required for Python programming
It's faster than all local setups
questions.explanation: Google Colab provides free cloud-based Jupyter notebooks with GPU access, making it ideal for ML learning without requiring expensive local hardware.
questions.learning_outcome: Understand cloud computing benefits for ML

questions.question_text: You need to install Python on your computer before using Google Colab.
questions.explanation: Google Colab runs entirely in the browser with Python pre-installed in the cloud environment. No local installation is needed.

questions.question_text: Name three Python libraries that are commonly pre-installed in Google Colab for machine learning.
questions.correct_answer: pandas, numpy, scikit-learn (or matplotlib, tensorflow, keras, seaborn, etc.)
questions.explanation: Colab comes with most popular data science and machine learning libraries pre-installed, including pandas for data manipulation, numpy for numerical computing, and scikit-learn for machine learning.

questions.question_text: Which of the 5 ML demos (stock predictor, cricket predictor, face detection, sentiment analyzer, chatbot) relates most to your industry or interests? Explain why in 2-3 sentences.
questions.explanation: There is no single correct answer. Reflect on your own professional context and interests. Consider how the demo's approach could be adapted to problems in your field.
questions.learning_outcome: Connect ML concepts to personal context

questions.question_text: You need to train a deep learning model on a large dataset, but your laptop only has a CPU and the training is very slow. What are three solutions you learned in this module to address this problem?
questions.correct_answer: 1) Use Google Colab with free GPU, 2) Enable GPU runtime in Colab settings (Runtime > Change runtime type > GPU), 3) Use cloud computing services like AWS/GCP with GPU instances
questions.explanation: This module introduced cloud-based solutions for GPU access. The most accessible for beginners is Google Colab, which provides free GPU access. You can also explore paid cloud platforms for more powerful GPU instances.
questions.learning_outcome: Apply cloud GPU solutions to real ML problems

options: A type of physical notebook for writing code
An interactive document that combines code, text, and visualizations
A cloud storage service for datasets
questions.explanation: A Jupyter notebook is an interactive document that allows you to combine executable code, rich text (markdown), visualizations, and outputs in a single document. It's widely used in data science and ML.
